what_it_does: Acceptance test that build_cognition_info projects six file-discovered corpus registries (lifters, mark_types, generation_policies, relation_types, ai_tics, forms) plus projections and spaces into the live cognition view, verifies registry-is-truth by dropping and removing a mark_type file, preserves the expanded role serialization, and checks the read-only floor.
observations: Defines a REGISTRIES mapping from contract keys to Suite attribute names for the six corpus registries.
Calls build_cognition_info directly with live registry objects and asserts each projected key equals the registry's own as_records() output.
Asserts schema_ver is at least 2 because the additive six-registry shape changes the serialization contract.
Tests that omitting all six registry kwargs still yields a valid shape with empty lists and no regression in prior keys.
Proves registry-is-truth by writing a new mark_types file, rediscovering, asserting the type appears, then removing it and asserting it disappears.
Verifies full-schema role fields (thinking, tools, knobs, context, model_binding) are projected present-only.
Performs a static floor scan on contracts.cognition_info source after stripping comments and docstrings, forbidding resolve/approve/dispatch/subprocess/os.system/claude -p tokens.
standards: File-drop/rediscover round-trip used as the live proof that the registry is the source of truth.
Direct contract-level equality assertion info[key] == reg.as_records() for six registries.
Static source-token floor scan that strips docstrings before scanning for forbidden action verbs.
conventions: Follows a runnable-script acceptance-test pattern with an ok() helper and a PASS counter.
Uses tempfile, FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite to build an isolated runtime for each run.
Imports modules with noqa: E402 after sys.path manipulation.
Breaks typical pytest convention by using a manual main harness instead of pytest test functions.
concerns: The static floor scan is brittle: naive docstring stripping may mis-handle nested triple quotes or f-strings containing triple quotes.
The test hardcodes the SCHEMA_VER >= 2 expectation, so it will fail if the version is not bumped even when the shape is present.
It couples tightly to exact Suite attribute names and registry methods (as_records, embeddable) without introspection fallback.
It reads the contracts.cognition_info source file at runtime, coupling the test to source layout.
Uses a global mutable PASS counter.
notes: A runnable acceptance harness that locks in the cognition-info contract's new six-registry projection and read-only floor.
questions: What exactly is the '+9 _serialize_role diff' that must be preserved?
How are the six registry directories discovered and what naming convention enforces the key-to-attribute mapping?
What does 'Group-L vector spaces' refer to for projections/spaces?
Is the static floor scan intended to replace runtime enforcement or only document intent?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it tests projection of six corpus registries, optional additive defaults, registry-is-truth, role diff preservation, and read-only floor.
apparent_themes: Read-only projection separating observation from action.
Acceptance-by-use rather than mocked unit testing.
intention_signals: The file is trying to guarantee that the live cognition view exposes every file-discovered corpus vocabulary exactly as the registries represent it, remains backward compatible when those registries are omitted, and stays a pure read surface with no operational verbs.
novelty: Combines a file-drop/rediscover round-trip to prove registry-is-truth with a static source-token floor scan for forbidden action verbs in the serializer.
contribution: A runnable acceptance harness that validates the cognition-info contract's six-registry projection, optional additive defaults, dynamic rediscovery behavior, full-schema role serialization, and read-only floor.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying that build_cognition_info projects six file-discovered corpus registries and projections/spaces verbatim, supports optional additive defaults, preserves expanded role fields, and maintains a read-only floor.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the live cognition view exposes all discovered corpus vocabularies registry-is-truth, stays backward compatible, and remains a pure read projection.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments PASS or fails the run.
symbols.description: ok(cond, label) checks a boolean condition, increments a global PASS counter and prints ok on success, or prints FAIL and raises SystemExit(1) on failure.

symbols.does: Builds a fresh Suite instance inside a temporary directory.
symbols.description: fresh_suite(tmp) creates an FsStore at the temp path, discovers the nodes directory with NodeRegistry, and returns a Suite for isolated test execution.

symbols.does: Executes the full acceptance test scenario.
symbols.description: run() performs the projection equality checks, optional-defaults checks, registry-is-truth file-drop round-trip, full-schema role diff preservation checks, and read-only floor scan, then prints the final PASS count.
